disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Three-month history of vague right-upper-quadrant discomfort, acutely worsened to a constant dull heavy ache over 48 hours
trigger_question_examples: Where is the pain and how long have you had it?
What is the pain like and has it changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a dull, heavy pressure up under my right ribs. It was a vague niggle for a few months but it's been constant for the last two days, which is what frightened me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Character of pain: dull, constant, heavy — NOT sharp, NOT colicky, NOT radiating
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain sharp or dull? Does it come in waves?
Does the pain move or spread anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's dull and constant, like a heavy pressure. It doesn't come in waves and it doesn't shoot anywhere.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negatives for an acute biliary/infective emergency: no fever, no rigors, no nausea or vomiting
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers, shivers or sweats?
Any nausea or vomiting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers or shivering at all, and I haven't felt sick or thrown up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negatives for jaundice/GI bleeding: no yellowing of skin or eyes noticed, no vomiting blood, stools appear normal (not black or bloody)
trigger_question_examples: Have you or anyone noticed your skin or the whites of your eyes going yellow?
Any black or tarry stools, or vomiting anything like blood or coffee grounds?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No yellowing that I've seen, and my bowel motions look normal — nothing black or bloody, and I haven't vomited any blood.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recent routine screening liver blood test reported abnormal by the patient's regular GP (values and interpretation not known to the patient)
trigger_question_examples: What prompted you to worry about your liver?
Have you had any recent blood tests?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My GP did routine bloods and said my liver test came back abnormal. I don't know the numbers — that's what set off my worry.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Background of poorly-controlled type 2 diabetes, dyslipidaemia and central obesity / metabolic syndrome
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any ongoing medical conditions?
What medications are you taking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got type 2 diabetes that hasn't been well controlled, high cholesterol, and I carry a lot of weight around my middle. I take metformin and atorvastatin.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Minimal alcohol intake and no viral-hepatitis risk factors reported
trigger_question_examples: How much alcohol do you drink?
Any risk factors for hepatitis — tattoos, injecting drug use, blood transfusions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I barely drink alcohol at all, and I've got no hepatitis risks that I know of.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dark, velvety skin patches on the posterior neck and axillae (patient-observable marker of insulin resistance)
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any changes to your skin, like dark patches?
Any darkening around the neck or armpits?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, yes — the skin on the back of my neck and under my arms has gone dark and a bit velvety.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe generalized fatigue over 3 months limiting exertion
trigger_question_examples: How has your energy been?
How is this affecting your day-to-day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been absolutely wiped out for months — the tiredness really limits how much I can do.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High health anxiety centred on fear of liver failure or liver cancer
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried this might be?
How are you feeling about all this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I'm terrified my liver is failing or that it's a tumour. I just need to know it's not something deadly.
